title: Countries with a low incidence of tuberculosis :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Countries with a low incidence of tuberculosis
You must have a chest X-ray if you have been living in a country that does not have a low incidence of tuberculosis (TB).

## When you need to have a chest X-ray
This page lists the countries that have a low incidence of TB.
If you are applying for a visa to come to New Zealand for 6 months or longer, you must have a chest X-ray if you:
- are a citizen of a country that is not on this list and have lived or spent time in that country, or
- have spent more than a total of 3 months in the past 5 years in any country that is not on this list. The 3 months can be made up of multiple countries and does not have to be continuous.
You may not need to have a chest X-ray if you:
- are a citizen of a country that is not on this list, and
- can prove you have never lived in or spent time in that country.
You are a citizen of Vietnam and Vietnam is not on this list. You must have a chest X-Ray unless you can prove you have never lived in or spent time in Vietnam.
You have spent more than 3 months in the last 5 years in countries that are not on this list. This was made up of:
- 5 weeks in one country last year
- 4 weeks in another country the year before, and
- 8 weeks in another country the year before that.
This means you must have a chest X-Ray.

## Check the list of countries with a low incidence of TB
- Bosnia and Herzegovina
- British Indian Ocean Territory
- British Virgin Islands
- Cocos (Keeling) Islands
- Heard and McDonald Islands
- Iran (Islamic Republic of)
- Occupied Palestinian Territory
- Saint Eustatius and Saba
- Saint Helena, Ascencion and Tristan da Cunha
- Saint Kitts and Nevis
- Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
- South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
- Svalbard and Jan Mayen
- Syrian Arab Republic
- United Arab Emirates
- United States of America
- United States Virgin Islands
- Wallis and Futuna Islands
